sections.paragraph:
φέγγος, ους, τό (Hom. Hymns+; ins, e.g. IAndrosIsis 39 [I b.c.]; LXX; TestJob 43:5; JosAs 14:9 φ. ἡλίου; GrBar; Ezek. Trag. 234 [Eus., PE 9, 29, 14] ἀπʼ οὐρανοῦ φ. 16; Philo; Jos., Ant. 2, 308; 11, 285; Tat. 20, 2) light, radiance, of the moon (Ps.-X., Cyneget. 5, 4; Philo, Somn. 1, 23) Mt 24:29; Mk 13:24. Of a λύχνος (Callim. 55, 3) Lk 11:33 v.l. (cp. TestJob 43:5 [w. λύχνος]). Of two heavenly beings πολὺ φέγγος ἔχοντες GPt 9:36.—DELG. Cp. φῶς.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

e.g. e.g. = exempli gratia (for example)

IAndrosIsis IAndrosIsis = Der Isishymnus von Andros und verwandte Texte—List 3

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

TestJob TestJob = Testament of Job—List 2

JosAs JosAs = Joseph and Aseneth—Lists 2, 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Trag.

Trag. = writer (s) of tragedy

trag. = tragedy, writer (s) of tragedy

Eus. Eus. = Eusebius of Caesarea, various works; IV a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Ps.-X Ps.-X , s. X.—List 5

Callim Callim , III b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 1051
